meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no marketing skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no marketing skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no marketing skills
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome no marketing skills. An intermediate checklist for students using Antigravity and Claude Code for success.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the lack of marketing skills requires shifting from 'promoting' to 'building in public.' By utilizing a robust SaaS starter kit, you automate the infrastructure, allowing you to focus on product-led growth. This approach transforms your technical project into a self-marketing engine that attracts users through transparency and utility.
content.intro:
You have the code, the logic, and the vision, but the abyss of 'no marketing' stands between your repository and your first paying user. It is a familiar trap for developers: building in silence, hoping the world discovers your brilliance. The reality is that code alone rarely speaks; it needs a megaphone built into its foundation.

This is where a high-performance SaaS starter kit becomes your unfair advantage. By engineering your application to be inherently discoverable and shareable, you bypass the need for traditional marketing. We will leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your development workflow into a content-generation machine, proving that the best marketing is simply an exceptional product.

steps.title: Automated SEO Architecture
steps.description: Configure your starter kit to handle dynamic metadata and Open Graph images automatically. This ensures every page you build is inherently shareable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure next-seo and dynamic Open Graph image generation for this starter kit. Ensure every route has a unique, SEO-friendly meta title and description.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'setup dynamic meta tags for all app routes'

steps.title: Public Changelog Integration
steps.description: Build an automated changelog that pushes updates to your landing page. This turns development progress into social media content.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a /changelog route that parses git commit messages into a user-friendly, public-facing feed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate changelog component from git logs'

steps.title: User-Led Referral Loops
steps.description: Implement a simple 'Invite' flow that rewards users for sharing. This builds a growth loop directly into your product UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a referral tracking system that generates unique share links and stores them in the database for new user attribution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement referral link generation and tracking logic'

steps.title: One-Click Demo Deployment
steps.description: Ensure your project is a 'one-click' experience for users. Frictionless access is the best marketing tool for intermediate developers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the authentication flow to support 'guest mode' or 'demo mode' for unauthenticated visitors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'add guest-access mode with limited functionality'

steps.title: Analytics Dashboard for Insights
steps.description: Embed lightweight analytics to see which features users actually engage with, informing your future roadmap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate posthog or plausible analytics into the starter kit to track user conversion events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'setup event tracking for signup and subscription buttons'

pro_tips: Treat your GitHub README as a landing page; use a high-quality demo video as the hero element.
Build a 'public roadmap' using a simple Trello or Notion board linked directly in your app footer.
Focus on 'utility-first' marketing: build free, small tools that solve one specific problem and link back to your main SaaS.
Use your commit history as a content calendar; share the 'how-I-built-this' story on X or LinkedIn every Friday.
Prioritize email capture over everything else; a newsletter is the only audience you truly own.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is configured, push your repository to GitHub, deploy to Vercel, and share your first 'Build in Public' thread on X tagging 3 other AI builders.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your code gather digital dust. By integrating growth mechanics directly into your development workflow, you stop being just a coder and start being a founder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
